fields.definition_origin: Brian Balfour (Reforge) — formalized TTV in growth-engineering curriculum 2017+; defined as 'time from install / signup to first perceived value'. Predecessor concepts: Sean Ellis 'must-have' threshold (40% rule, Hacking Growth 2017) and Andrew Chen's first-action latency analysis at Uber/Andreessen Horowitz. Industry-codified as a primary growth metric by Reforge, ProductLed, Eleken, OpenView, and the 2024-2026 PLG canon. Distinguished from Time-to-Aha (TTA — realization) and Time-to-Activation (TTA — behavior); TTV is the umbrella term encompassing both.
fields.core_mechanism: TTV measures the latency between user intent (install/signup) and the first moment the user perceives value. Shorter TTV = faster Aha + Activation = higher retention. Industry consensus target for consumer mobile is <60 seconds for short-onboarding apps (Linear, Cron, Stripe Atlas onboarding, Acorns) — anything over 2-3 minutes loses the impatient majority. Long-onboarding apps (Cal AI, Mau Baron's Prayer Lock) appear to violate TTV at 10-15 minutes — but they don't violate it, they REDEFINE it: TTV becomes the AI plan reveal moment (synthetic Aha at step N-1), and the user perceives ongoing value accruing across the questionnaire labor (each step is itself a small value moment because it visibly threads into the reveal). The mechanism is dual: (1) for short-onboarding apps, minimize literal latency from install to first action; (2) for long-onboarding apps, distribute perceived value across the questionnaire so the user feels TTV is short even though clock time is long. Front-loading the value PROMISE (screen 1 anchor) lets the user tolerate long onboarding because the promise is felt to be coming.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Anticipation of perceived value engages ventral striatum reward signaling (Knutson et al. 2001). Short TTV produces a dopamine hit close in time to the install action — strengthening the install-as-cause-of-value association. Long TTV without value cues triggers anterior insula loss-aversion processing — the user starts encoding install as 'I might have wasted my time'. The synthetic-TTV trick of long-onboarding apps works because each questionnaire answer fires a small ventral striatum response (the answer matters → the reveal is being built) — keeping the dopamine cadence high across 10-15 minutes.
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) Eleken case studies (industry blog 2024) — Linear's TTV ~30 seconds (sign-up + open first issue); Cron / Notion Calendar's TTV <1 minute; Stripe Atlas TTV <90 seconds. (2) Reforge curriculum (Balfour 2017+) — TTV as primary growth metric in 600+ SaaS case studies. (3) Industry mobile data: apps with TTV >5 min on short-onboarding patterns lose 60%+ of installs at first session. (4) Counter-data: long-onboarding apps (Cal AI 33-step → AI reveal) achieve 'effective TTV' at the reveal moment (~10 min in) and outperform short-onboarding apps on D7/D30 retention by 20-40% (Mau Baron / Cal AI public reports). (5) RevenueCat 2025: AI-wrapper trial conversion 5.31% — lower than non-AI 10.92% — but +14% direct purchase, suggesting longer onboarding produces stronger value-perception per user. (6) Adapty 2026: 90% of trial starts on Day 0; 44.5% of all purchases on Day 0 — meaning TTV optimization at Day 0 is the dominant lever. (7) Industry rules of thumb: <60s for utility/social/fintech, <5min for AI-wrapper short, 10-15min OK for wellness/habit AI-wrapper IF synthetic-TTV is engineered.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: TTV (median time-from-install to first-perceived-value-event in seconds), TTV distribution (long tail = problem cohort), install-to-Aha latency, install-to-Activation latency, install-to-paywall-shown latency, install-to-trial-started latency, abandonment-by-elapsed-time-bucket (D0 install → 30s, 60s, 5min, 15min, 30min cohorts).
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Eleken case studies: Linear's <30s TTV correlates with their best-in-class D30 retention (>50% for B2B SaaS). Reforge: each 30s reduction in TTV at the <2min range correlates with ~5% D7 retention lift in surveyed apps. Long-onboarding synthetic-TTV apps (Cal AI, Headway) achieve 20-40% higher D30 retention than short-onboarding equivalents in their categories. Adapty 2026: 90% of trial starts on Day 0 — TTV optimization compounds because most purchase decisions happen on install day.
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) For short-onboarding apps: literally measure install-to-first-tap, install-to-first-action, install-to-Aha. Profile cold-start to hit interactive within 1.5s. Use SDUI + bundled assets to skip splash. Pre-warm fonts. Use deferred deep-link to route directly to relevant onboarding variant (skip generic welcome). (2) For long-onboarding apps: front-load the value PROMISE on screen 1 (item 3 anchor). Distribute value cues across questionnaire (each answer visibly threads into reveal — cross-step stitching). Engineer perceived-TTV at the AI plan reveal step. (3) Cohort segmentation: route different ad cohorts to different onboarding variants with different TTV characteristics (impatient TikTok cohort → shorter variant; patient organic cohort → longer narrative variant). (4) Telemetry: instrument TTV at the event level (install_received_at, first_screen_paint_at, first_tap_at, ai_plan_reveal_at, paywall_shown_at, trial_started_at) — compute deltas in dashboard. (5) Latency masking: theatrical loader (item 13) at the AI plan reveal lets you hide LLM latency (1-3s) inside engineered loader narrative (4-7s). (6) Offline-first: ensure first screen paints with cached assets even on cold network — TTV must not depend on network. (7) For onboarding paywall: TTV should arrive 1-2 seconds before the paywall, not simultaneously (let the user feel value before seeing price).
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Linear sign-up to first issue: ~30 seconds. Sign-up form is minimal (Google SSO), workspace setup is opinionated default, first issue creation prompt is the Aha + Activation collapse. (2) Cron (Notion Calendar): TTV <60s — sign-up + calendar connect + first event view. (3) Stripe Atlas company-formation: TTV ~90s — first three fields collected, immediate 'Your Delaware LLC will be ready in 4 days' value-promise. (4) Cal AI: 'effective TTV' ~10 min — 33 questions, AI reveal at step ~30. Synthetic TTV via questionnaire labor + reveal. (5) Headway: 'effective TTV' ~5 min — 8 questions, personalized summary feed reveal. (6) Acorns: TTV = bank-account-link time (~3-5 min in practice due to bank OAuth) — the entire onboarding success metric. (7) Mau Baron Prayer Lock: 'effective TTV' ~15 min — narrative arc + AI prayer reveal. (8) Anti-example: an AI-wrapper that opens with empty chat input and no example prompts — TTV depends on user inventing first prompt; can be 30s for power users, never for everyone else.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile cold-start time + permission gates + ATT prompts add to TTV in ways web doesn't have. Mobile users also have higher install regret rate (uninstall within first session) — TTV pressure is greater. Web has lower install friction (no install at all in some cases) but slower context switch (tab vs app). Mobile-primary for synthetic TTV via long onboarding. Web equivalent: streamlined sign-up + immediate workspace/feature engagement (Linear, Notion, Vercel are best-in-class).
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Aligned with both. Apple Liquid Glass (iOS 26+) bolder typography + faster-feeling transitions help short-TTV apps; translucent material + dynamic morphing help long-TTV apps feel premium. Material 3 Expressive 4x faster element recognition (18k-participant study) directly reduces literal TTV by accelerating user comprehension. Both platforms reward TTV-optimized design.
fields.category_fit: Universal — every category cares about TTV. Short-TTV (<60s) critical for: utility (calculator), social (Instagram), fintech (Acorns), B2B productivity (Linear, Notion), pure consumer (Tinder, Spotify). Synthetic long-TTV (10-15 min effective) acceptable for: wellness/habit AI-wrapper (Cal AI, Flo, Noom, Headspace AI), faith/lifestyle (Prayer Lock, Hallow), edtech (Headway, Blinkist). DO NOT use long synthetic-TTV for utility apps (users don't want to wait), B2B (users want to start working), social (users want to post). DO NOT use short-TTV for AI-wrapper wellness — you sacrifice the synthetic Aha lift.
fields.tooling_options: PostHog (TTV funnel + cohort comparison + A/B test), Mixpanel / Amplitude (TTV analytics dashboards), Sentry / Bugsnag (cold-start performance), Firebase Performance Monitoring (mobile cold-start), AppsFlyer / Adjust / Branch (deferred deep-link routing for cohort-variant TTV). For LLM-Aha latency masking: Vercel AI SDK + theatrical loader, OpenAI/Anthropic streaming responses.
fields.ai_dependency: optional: short-TTV apps (Linear, Acorns) have no AI dependency. Synthetic long-TTV apps (Cal AI, Prayer Lock) require LLM for the reveal moment. When AI is used: GPT-4-class reasoning, ~1-3s latency masked by 4-7s theatrical loader, offline-fallback static reveal mandatory.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude to instrument and optimize TTV explicitly. Example fragment 1: 'Implement TTV telemetry. Required events: install_received, first_screen_paint, first_tap, ai_plan_reveal_started, ai_plan_reveal_completed, paywall_shown, trial_started, first_core_action_completed. In analytics, compute deltas: TTV-to-first-paint, TTV-to-first-tap, TTV-to-Aha, TTV-to-Activation, TTV-to-paywall. Build a cohort dashboard segmenting by ad-source and onboarding-variant. Target: median TTV-to-first-paint <1.5s on iPhone SE / Pixel 6a.' Example fragment 2: 'For the cold-start path: bundle Cabinet Grotesk + Manrope as inline fonts (no remote fetch on first paint), preload onboarding variant JSON with deferred deep-link, render screen 1 within 1.5s of install on iPhone SE class hardware. The first screen MUST paint with brand type, not iOS system fallback flash. Forbid splash screens >800ms.'
fields.stitch_application: Stitch must be told to design for fast-feeling first paint. Example fragment 1: 'Onboarding screen 1 must feel arrived-at within ~1 second of opening. Design: full-bleed warm cinematic photo (NOT abstract gradient — gradients feel slow), top-anchored 32pt Cabinet Grotesk Bold problem-statement headline, single primary CTA pinned bottom thumb zone. NO loading state on first screen — it must paint with assets. NO splash screen with logo lock-up >500ms.' Example fragment 2: 'For long-onboarding apps: design a screen-1 promise that signals \'value is ~10 minutes away\' so users tolerate the questionnaire. Microcopy: \'In 5 minutes, you\'ll have a personalized plan made for you.\' Include a small visual cue (3-stat illustration or mock-plan card preview) so the value is partially anchored before the questionnaire begins.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen defaults to: (1) splash screen with logo lock-up + Lottie animation (3-5s wasted before first interactive screen); (2) generic 'Welcome' first screen with no value promise (anchor + TTV both fail); (3) no TTV instrumentation (only 'onboarding_completed' event); (4) loading the heaviest assets first (icon font + onboarding video + analytics SDK) blocking first paint; (5) cold-start network call to fetch onboarding config (TTV depends on network). Counter-prompt: 'TTV-to-first-paint must be <1.5s on iPhone SE / Pixel 6a. Forbid splash screens >800ms. Forbid network call before first interactive screen — bundle onboarding config locally with deferred deep-link variant override. Inline Cabinet Grotesk + Manrope so brand type paints on first frame. Instrument explicit TTV events: install_received, first_screen_paint, first_tap, ai_plan_reveal_completed. Compute TTV-to-Aha and TTV-to-Activation in analytics dashboard.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Splash screen >800ms — pure TTV waste. (2) Network-dependent first paint (waiting on remote config / asset / SSO before showing screen 1). (3) Heavy first-screen with 4 video / 6 illustrations / 12 features (TTV bloat). (4) Generic 'Welcome' anchor (the user can't perceive value yet). (5) AI plan reveal latency unmasked (LLM call shows generic spinner without theatrical loader narrative). (6) Long onboarding without value distribution (questions feel like data extraction, not value building). (7) Measuring only 'onboarding_completed' (no TTV granularity). (8) Conflating TTV with onboarding-length (long onboarding can have great TTV if value is distributed; short onboarding can have poor TTV if value isn't perceived).
fields.regulatory_risk: Low. TTV optimization itself is pro-user. Minor risk: aggressive TTV-cutting that hides legal disclosures (auto-renewal, data minimization). FTC Click-to-Cancel (2025-07-14) requires onboarding subscription disclosure to mirror cancellation pathway — TTV optimization can't hide the disclosure. EU Digital Fairness Act (Q4 2026 draft) flags fast-track flows that bypass informed consent.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Apple HIG explicitly recommends fast launch. Apple Guideline 4.0 — design polish; sloppy splash screens have been cited in rejections. Material 3 Expressive ship-faster guidance includes recognition speed (TTV proxy). Google Play Pre-Launch Reports include cold-start latency. ATT prompt timing affects TTV — recommended after value is shown, not before.
fields.accessibility_implications: Fast TTV must not bypass accessibility. Screen 1 must be VoiceOver/TalkBack accessible from first paint (set accessibilityLabel on the headline, primary CTA, skip). Dynamic-type to 200% must not break the first-paint layout. prefers-reduced-motion respect on first-screen entrance animation. WCAG 2.2 §1.4.3 contrast on first paint must hold even before lazy-loaded brand assets land.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) TTV optimization fights with rich onboarding (long-onboarding paradox in item 4) — they're reconcilable via synthetic-TTV but requires sophistication. (2) TTV measurement noise: install timing varies by store delivery (~seconds to minutes); use first-screen-paint as the practical zero. (3) Cold-start performance varies sharply on low-end Android (Pixel 6a class) vs flagship — measure on minimum-spec hardware. (4) Network-flap recovery: TTV must hold under poor network (cached assets + offline-first first paint). (5) Cohort segmentation: paid TikTok cohort tolerates different TTV than organic cohort — A/B test by source. (6) Optimal TTV varies by category — don't apply 60s rule to wellness AI-wrapper. (7) Permission/ATT gates are TTV poison if cold-asked early — defer until after value.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: TTV pressure is largely universal — impatience varies but every market punishes slow first-paint. Cultural calibration: US/Western markets expect <2s first paint; LATAM/SEA tolerate slightly slower due to network norms; CN expects fast paint with high data density. Network-quality varies sharply (4G vs 5G coverage) — TTV must be tested in low-bandwidth conditions. RTL languages: first-paint layout must be RTL-correct from frame 0, not flipped on second render.
fields.prescribed_action: Instrument TTV explicitly with event-level granularity (install → first-paint → first-tap → Aha → Activation → paywall → trial), target <1.5s first-paint and <60s TTV-to-Aha for short-onboarding apps, or engineer synthetic-TTV at the AI plan reveal (10-15 min effective TTV with value distributed across questionnaire) for long-onboarding apps. Forbid splash screens >800ms and network-dependent first paint.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: For long-onboarding questionnaires, TTV is engineered synthetically by distributing value across each step. Each question must visibly thread into the reveal (cross-step stitching) so the user perceives value being built. Sequencing: low-friction → identity → goals → painpoint → metrics → reveal. Screen 1 must promise the value (~10 min away) so users tolerate the labor.
fields.aha_relevance: TTV-to-Aha is the primary metric for long-onboarding apps. The synthetic Aha at step N-1 IS the TTV target. Short-onboarding apps collapse TTV-to-Aha and TTV-to-Activation; long-onboarding apps separate them. Both need explicit measurement.
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Universal — TTV applies to every archetype. Strongest for Magician (transformation must arrive within tolerable time), Hero (mastery realization), Caregiver (gentle but present value). Weakest tension with Sage (knowledge depth tolerates longer TTV) and Lover (relatedness builds slowly). Pairs with .agent/research/emotional-design/results/Jungian_Brand_Archetypes.json.
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Splash Screen Bloat (TTV waste); Wasted Loading Screens (loader without narrative kills perceived TTV); Generic Welcome (TTV is wasted on a screen that conveys no value); Network-Dependent First Paint (TTV depends on network conditions); No Telemetry (you can't optimize what you don't measure).
